evidence: String '0004 NIT 00' appears in DB text at line 5, immediately before '2024 ND 68'. Full PDF scan finds NO occurrence of '0004 NIT 00' anywhere. This is a metadata/docket artifact from OCR that does not appear in the authoritative PDF.

evidence: At end of ¶4 in DB: 'remain unresolved. П' (Cyrillic П = U+041F, Latin Letter P). PDF at same location shows: 'remain unresolved.' (period), then blank line, then 'II' (Roman numeral) centered on its own line. The Cyrillic П is a misrecognition of Roman numeral II, and the joined structure violates PDF layout where section marker stands alone.
note: High-risk OCR error: Cyrillic character confused with Roman numeral. Also violates paragraph structure (section marker should be separate block).

evidence: DB has section marker I joined to end of paragraph: 'We dismiss the appeal. I\n[¶2]...'. PDF shows: 'We dismiss the appeal.' then blank line, then 'I' centered on its own line (line 58 in PDF), then blank line before next paragraph. Section markers in ND opinions stand alone.
note: Ingestion artifact: section marker wrongly joined to preceding sentence instead of being separated.

evidence: DB has section marker III joined to end of paragraph: 'there is no right to appeal. III\n[¶8]...'. PDF shows: 'there is no right to appeal.' then blank line, then 'III' centered on its own line (line 151 in PDF), then blank line before next paragraph. Same structural error as Issue 3.
note: Ingestion artifact: section marker wrongly joined to preceding sentence instead of being separated.

note: Caption paragraph structure correction. In a multi-party caption, 'Respondents' should label the parties already listed (father and child) before 'and' introduces the additional mother party. Content of all opinions and citations verified against PDF source—all cases, docket numbers, and factual findings match.
